metadata.archetype_justification: This is a desktop Windows application with no persistent data store, no server-side API surface, no database connections, and no inter-service communication. All operations are local, synchronous, and user-initiated. Classified as stateless-utility.

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No infrastructure as code exists — all infrastructure (if any) is manual
top_gaps.impact: No reproducible infrastructure; no disaster recovery capability

top_gaps.title: Monolith vs Microservices
top_gaps.gap: Tightly coupled monolithic desktop application with no module boundaries for cloud decomposition
top_gaps.impact: Cannot independently scale, deploy, or modernize components

top_gaps.title: Integration Testing
top_gaps.gap: Zero automated tests of any kind (unit or integration)
top_gaps.impact: No safety net for any migration or modernization effort; high regression risk

top_gaps.title: Application Security Pipeline
top_gaps.gap: No SAST, DAST, or dependency vulnerability scanning in CI/CD pipeline
top_gaps.impact: Vulnerabilities in dependencies may reach release artifacts undetected

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No cloud compute — application is distributed as a desktop installer
top_gaps.impact: No cloud elasticity, no managed patching, no operational visibility

approaches.name: Strangler Fig (Parallel Track)
approaches.recommendation: Recommended. Keep the desktop app as a client; build backend services incrementally.

approaches.name: Conditional / Adaptive
approaches.recommendation: Recommended when starting with a single cloud feature.

approaches.name: Big-Bang Rewrite
approaches.recommendation: Recommended against. The desktop app serves its users well; focus on adding cloud capabilities.

findings.title: No managed compute — desktop application with no cloud infrastructure
findings.description: Greenshot is distributed as a Windows desktop application (WinExe). There is no cloud compute infrastructure — no EC2, ECS, EKS, Lambda, or Fargate. Build compute uses GitHub-hosted runners but these are ephemeral CI/CD resources, not production infrastructure.
findings.gap: No cloud compute exists. The application has no server-side component and no managed compute services.
findings.recommendation: If cloud features are planned (image hosting, collaboration), provision managed compute using EKS (preferred per technology preferences) for backend services.

findings.title: No network security infrastructure — desktop application
findings.description: No VPC, security groups, NACLs, or network segmentation exist. The application is a desktop client with no cloud networking infrastructure. Network communication consists of outbound HTTPS calls to third-party APIs with TLS 1.2/1.3 enforced.
findings.gap: No cloud network security infrastructure exists. If backend services are added, network security must be designed from scratch.
findings.recommendation: When cloud services are introduced, deploy them in a VPC with private subnets, least-privilege security groups, and VPC endpoints.

findings.title: No API entry point — no gateway or load balancer
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, CloudFront, or any managed entry point exists. The application is a desktop client that makes outbound HTTP calls but exposes no API.
findings.gap: No API entry point exists. No managed traffic control, throttling, or authentication layer.
findings.recommendation: If cloud backend services are developed, place API Gateway in front with throttling, authentication, and request validation.

findings.title: No auto-scaling — no cloud compute to scale
findings.description: No auto-scaling exists. The application runs on end-user desktops with no cloud compute that could be scaled.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling configuration for any resource.
findings.recommendation: When cloud services are introduced, configure auto-scaling from the start — EKS HPA for compute, DynamoDB auto-scaling for data.

findings.title: Zero IaC coverage — no infrastructure defined as code
findings.description: No infrastructure as code exists in the repository. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm, or Kustomize configurations. The only infrastructure-like definition is the GitHub Actions workflow configuration for CI/CD runners.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage. All infrastructure (if any exists beyond GitHub-hosted resources) is manually created.
findings.recommendation: If cloud infrastructure is introduced, adopt IaC from the start using CDK or Terraform. Define GitHub Pages and Cloudflare configurations as IaC.

findings.title: Legacy .NET Framework 4.8 — limited cloud-native tooling
findings.description: C# targeting .NET Framework 4.8 (net480) with LangVersion=latest. Uses Windows Forms and WPF interop. Heavy use of Windows-specific APIs: DirectX, COM Interop (Office 15.x), P/Invoke, WinRT. The .NET SDK 9.0.311 is used for build tooling but runtime target is legacy .NET Framework.
findings.gap: .NET Framework 4.8 is legacy — no cross-platform support, cannot run in Linux containers, narrower cloud-native tooling ecosystem compared to modern .NET 8/9.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate migration to .NET 8/9. Windows-specific UI (WinForms/WPF) can target net8.0-windows but gains modern .NET libraries, better performance, and improved cloud tooling.

findings.title: Tightly coupled monolith — single deployable desktop application
findings.description: Greenshot is a single deployable unit (WinExe) with 12 projects compiled into one application. Plugin architecture exists but all code runs in a single process with shared static state and configuration. Core modules have no defined interfaces or boundaries.
findings.gap: Tightly-coupled monolith with no clear module boundaries for cloud decomposition. Pervasive shared state through static classes.
findings.recommendation: If cloud modernization is planned, identify service boundaries within the plugin architecture. Existing plugins are natural candidates for extraction into cloud-mediated services.

findings.title: No API versioning — desktop app exposes no API
findings.description: No API versioning exists. Greenshot is a desktop application that does not expose any API. It consumes third-party APIs but does not provide one.
findings.gap: No API exists to version. If backend services are developed, versioning must be designed from the start.
findings.recommendation: When building cloud APIs, implement URL-path versioning (e.g., /v1/) from the first release.

findings.title: No service discovery — hardcoded third-party endpoints
findings.description: No service discovery mechanism exists. Third-party service endpoints are hardcoded as constants in plugin source code. No dynamic discovery, no registry, no configuration-driven endpoint resolution.
findings.gap: All external service endpoints are hardcoded constants. No dynamic discovery or service mesh.
findings.recommendation: When building cloud backend services, implement service discovery (AWS Cloud Map) from the start.

findings.title: Local file system storage — no cloud object storage
findings.description: Screenshots and annotations are stored on the local file system in user-specified directories. No cloud object storage, no managed storage service, no parsing pipeline.
findings.gap: Data on local file systems with no cloud accessibility. Cloud storage (S3) needed for cross-device sync or shared features.
findings.recommendation: For cloud storage features, store images in S3 with lifecycle policies. Consider Textract for OCR on screenshots.

findings.title: Custom INI-based config — non-standard data access
findings.description: The application uses a custom INI-based configuration system (IniConfig) and direct System.IO APIs for file operations. No repository pattern or formal abstraction layer.
findings.gap: Data access through custom, non-standard INI system and direct file I/O. No storage abstraction layer.
findings.recommendation: Implement a storage abstraction layer (repository pattern) that can target both local file system and S3 interchangeably.

findings.title: No cloud audit logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail or cloud audit logging exists. log4net is used for local application logging only. Logs are written to local files with no tamper-proof storage.
findings.gap: No cloud audit logging. Local logs are not audit-grade.
findings.recommendation: When cloud services are introduced, enable CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 Object Lock.

findings.title: No API authentication — no API exists
findings.description: No API exists to authenticate. The application is a desktop client using OAuth2 for outbound auth to third-party services, but exposes no inbound API.
findings.gap: No API authentication because no API exists. Backend services would need auth designed from scratch.
findings.recommendation: When building backend APIs, implement OAuth2/JWT authentication on all endpoints. Consider Amazon Cognito.

findings.title: No centralized identity provider
findings.description: No centralized IdP integration. Each plugin manages its own OAuth authentication independently (Box, Dropbox, Imgur, Confluence, Jira).
findings.gap: Per-plugin independent authentication with no unified identity layer.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito as centralized IdP. Enable SSO for cloud-connected features.

findings.title: No compute hardening or vulnerability scanning
findings.description: No compute hardening or patching strategy. No vulnerability scanning. CI/CD uses windows-latest GitHub runners maintained by GitHub but no scanning of build environment or dependencies.
findings.gap: No vulnerability scanning (Inspector, Snyk). No hardened base images.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot for NuGet packages. If cloud compute is introduced, use hardened base images and enable AWS Inspector.

findings.title: No security scanning in CI/CD pipeline
findings.description: No SAST, DAST, or dependency scanning in the pipeline. No Dependabot, no dotnet list package --vulnerable, no SonarQube. Pipeline builds and packages without security validation.
findings.gap: No security scanning of any kind. NuGet dependency vulnerabilities could reach release artifacts undetected.
findings.recommendation: Enable Dependabot for NuGet. Add dotnet list package --vulnerable as CI step. Consider Semgrep for SAST.

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: No distributed tracing instrumented. Only log4net for local logging. No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or tracing SDK.
findings.gap: No tracing. For a desktop app this is expected, but cloud services would need tracing.
findings.recommendation: When cloud services are introduced, instrument OpenTelemetry with X-Ray integration.

findings.title: No business metrics or telemetry
findings.description: No custom metrics published. No CloudWatch, no telemetry, no analytics. No visibility into application usage patterns.
findings.gap: No metrics collection or usage visibility.
findings.recommendation: Implement opt-in telemetry. For cloud features, publish business metrics to CloudWatch.

findings.title: No anomaly detection or alerting
findings.description: No anomaly detection or alerting exists. No monitoring infrastructure, no CloudWatch alarms, no error rate tracking.
findings.gap: No alerting or anomaly detection.
findings.recommendation: When cloud services are introduced, configure CloudWatch anomaly detection on error rates and latency.

findings.title: Basic deployment — direct publish with pre-release flag
findings.description: Deployments publish directly to GitHub Releases and Chocolatey. Pre-release flag provides minimal staging but no canary, blue/green, or gradual rollout.
findings.gap: No staged rollout. All users receive the same version simultaneously.
findings.recommendation: Implement a beta channel. Consider auto-update with staged rollout.

findings.title: Zero automated tests — no unit or integration tests
findings.description: No automated tests exist. The solution has 12 projects, none of which are test projects. No test framework dependencies exist.
findings.gap: Zero test coverage. Most critical gap for any modernization effort — extreme regression risk.
findings.recommendation: Add xUnit/NUnit test project. Start with unit tests for core image processing, then integration tests for plugin APIs.

findings.title: No incident response automation or runbooks
findings.description: No incident response automation. No runbooks, no documented process for handling release issues.
findings.gap: No incident response process.
findings.recommendation: Document release rollback process. When cloud services are added, implement automated runbooks.

findings.title: No observability ownership
findings.description: No observability ownership model. No dashboards, no named alarm owners, no CODEOWNERS file.
findings.gap: No observability ownership of any kind.
findings.recommendation: Add CODEOWNERS file. Establish per-service dashboards when cloud services are added.

findings.title: No resource tagging — no AWS resources exist
findings.description: No AWS resource tagging because no AWS resources exist. Desktop tool with no cloud infrastructure to tag.
findings.gap: No tagging governance for future cloud resources.
findings.recommendation: When cloud infrastructure is introduced, establish tagging standard and enforce via IaC.

findings.title: CI/CD pipeline lacks testing stage
findings.description: GitHub Actions CI/CD is well-implemented with 4 workflows covering build, package, and deploy stages. Build secrets are properly managed via GitHub Secrets. However, no automated testing stage exists in the pipeline.
findings.gap: No automated testing stage in the pipeline — the build proceeds directly from compile to package without any test execution. No IaC deployment automation (no infrastructure to deploy).
findings.recommendation: Add a test execution stage between build and package in the release workflow. Integrate Dependabot and a SAST tool as additional pipeline stages.

findings.title: Secrets managed but no rotation mechanism
findings.description: Build-time secrets (OAuth client IDs and secrets for Box, Dropbox, Imgur) are managed via GitHub Secrets and injected as environment variables during CI/CD. Template files contain placeholder variables, not actual secrets. No plaintext credentials exist in the repository.
findings.gap: Secrets are properly managed in CI/CD via GitHub Secrets, but there is no rotation mechanism. OAuth client credentials are static — no automated rotation is configured. No dedicated secrets management service is in use.
findings.recommendation: For current desktop app context, GitHub Secrets is appropriate. If cloud services are introduced, migrate to AWS Secrets Manager with automated rotation for all service credentials.

triggering_questions.note: Tightly coupled monolith with no module boundaries

detail.current_state: Monolithic desktop application with no cloud compute
immediate_actions: Identify service boundaries in plugin architecture

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1<3 and no container definitions found, BUT contextual guard blocks: desktop application with no server-side workload to containerize

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold but contextual guard prevents trigger — no server-side workload exists to containerize

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Desktop application with no server-side workload. There is no EC2/VM-based compute to containerize — the application runs on end-user machines.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (≥3); no commercial database engines detected

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures; all logic in application layer. Threshold not met.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=Not Evaluated (no persistent data store); no databases to migrate

triggering_questions.note: Not Evaluated — no persistent data store exists. Cannot trigger.

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No database of any kind exists in this system. Surface flag has_persistent_data_store=false.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4=Not Evaluated (stateless-utility archetype); no data processing workloads exist

triggering_questions.note: Not Evaluated — stateless-utility archetype. No streaming or analytics artifacts found.

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No data processing workloads exist. Contextual guard blocks — desktop application with no streaming, ETL, or analytics infrastructure.

triggering_questions.note: Rolling deployment with pre-release flag only

detail.current_state: CI/CD exists (INF-Q11=3) but no IaC, no tests, basic deployment strategy

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context describes 'Windows screenshot and annotation tool' with no AI-related signal terms.
